title: linaria villosa subsp. nummularia
intro: Linaria villosa subsp. nummularia, also known as nummularia toadflax, is a plant species within the toadflax genus, characterized by its hairy stems and blue-purple flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and acting as an antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Linaria villosa subsp. nummularia can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
